generated_by: Claude (Sonnet 5), refined from ChatGPT first drafts - see prompts.md

test_cases.title: Login with valid credentials returns a usable auth token
test_cases.expected_result: 200 response; body includes a non-empty access token and matches the requesting user's identity

test_cases.title: Login with invalid password is rejected
steps: POST /auth/login with a valid username and an incorrect password

test_cases.title: Protected endpoint rejects requests with no token
steps: GET a protected/authenticated endpoint with no Authorization header

test_cases.title: Protected endpoint accepts a valid token
steps: Log in to obtain a token
GET a protected endpoint with 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'
test_cases.expected_result: 200 response; returned data matches the authenticated user

test_cases.title: Create resource (POST) returns the created object with an id
steps: POST a new resource with valid fields
test_cases.expected_result: 201 response; body echoes submitted fields and includes a new id

test_cases.title: Read resource (GET by id) returns the correct object
steps: GET a resource by a known valid id
test_cases.expected_result: 200 response; returned id matches requested id

test_cases.title: Update resource (PUT) persists and returns the change
steps: PUT an existing resource with an updated field
test_cases.expected_result: 200 response; returned object reflects the updated field
test_cases.note: Against a fake backend (see README), 'persists' is only verified via the response contract, not a follow-up GET, since writes aren't actually stored.

test_cases.title: Delete resource (DELETE) removes it
steps: DELETE an existing resource by id
test_cases.expected_result: 200/204 response; a subsequent GET for that id returns 404
test_cases.note: Follow-up-GET-returns-404 half of this case isn't checkable against a fake backend that doesn't persist deletes; the delete-response-contract half is.

test_cases.title: GET a nonexistent resource returns 404
steps: GET a resource id that does not exist
test_cases.expected_result: 404 Not Found with an informative error body

test_cases.title: Malformed request body returns 4xx, not 5xx
steps: POST /auth/login with a non-JSON body and Content-Type: application/json
test_cases.expected_result: 4xx response; server does not crash or return a raw 500/stack trace

test_cases.title: Missing required field returns 4xx with a field-specific error
steps: POST /auth/login with the password field omitted
test_cases.expected_result: 4xx response; error body indicates which field is missing/invalid

test_cases.title: List endpoint enforces rate limiting under burst traffic
steps: Send N requests to the same endpoint in rapid succession
test_cases.expected_result: Once the rate limit threshold is crossed, the API returns 429 with a Retry-After header rather than degrading, hanging, or 500ing
test_cases.note: The demo API doesn't enforce a documented rate limit, so this only asserts 'fails safely' rather than a real 429+Retry-After contract. See prompts.md API notes and README known limitations.

test_cases.title: Response schema matches the documented contract
steps: Validate each item against the required field set and types
test_cases.expected_result: Every item has all required fields with the correct types; no field is silently null where a value is expected

test_cases.title: Auth response schema includes all fields the client depends on
test_cases.expected_result: Response includes id, username, email, accessToken, and refreshToken
